title: Malt Extract Broth and agarized concentrated
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ai3t7","text":"Recipe and protocol for Concentrated Malt Extract Broth medium preparation. An optional step for Agarization is provided.   ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"7jn6b","text":"Malt extract (technical grade) 30 g/L","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"43cpv","text":"Glucose (technical grade) 10 g/L","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a9kd3","text":"Tryptone/Peptone powder (technical grade) 5 g/L","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d7iuj","text":"Yeast extract (technical grade)  3 g/L","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bsmou","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f2vdc","text":"Agar (optional) 15 g/L","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c0cnv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eltm7","text":"Milli Q or Tap water to volume 1L.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Take note of the position of YourFavouriteIngredients and take them from the reagent-room.

Weight ingredients: Malt extract (technical grade) 30 g/L Glucose (technical grade) 10 g/L Tryptone/Peptone powder (technical grade) 5 g/L Yeast extract (technical grade) 3 g/L Agar (optional step) 15 g/L Milli Q or Tap water to volume 1L.

Add all the solid ingredients to a plastic or glass becker, along with 600-700mL of water to dissolve the ingredients. In this step is facultative to measure Water with a cilinder. Its facultative to add autoclavable components (vitamins, antibiotics etc.)

Agarization step facultative Add 15 g/L Agar microbiology grade along with 100mL of water to "dissolve" the agar. It will settle to the bottom due to low solubilization below 50°C, it will be mixed during autoclaving step.
Finalise your mixture Make up to final 1L volume with remaining water and further mix.

Autoclave it! Pour the content into a Duran/Pyrex bottle to autoclave the mixture. Do not fill the whole bottle , leave some headspace (1/4 of total volume HS).Do not seal the bottle , leave the top half-round open. Remember to place autoclave - tape over your bottles. Autoclave setting: 121°C for 15min, 15psi/1bar
creator: Marta Puddu
